excerpt: The daʿwa exists to forestall the regret of the grave by gathering the soul to righteous deed while it still possesses agency in the world.
source_location_hint: on the regret of the grave and the urgency of preparation

excerpt: The supplications of Imam Zayn al-ʿAbidin describe the believer's heart trembling at the verses of chastisement and yearning at the verses of mercy.
source_location_hint: supplication on completing the recitation of the Qurʾān

excerpt: The first perfection of any act of worship is its outward form; the second perfection is its sealing in walāya — without the seal, the form yields no fruit.
source_location_hint: on kamāl awwal and kamāl thānī

excerpt: The beginning of anger is madness and its end is regret; whoever does not regret has persisted in his madness.
source_authority: Amir al-Muʾminin ʿAli ibn Abi Talib

excerpt: Forgiveness when one has the power to retaliate is the zakāt of victory.
source_authority: Amir al-Muʾminin ʿAli ibn Abi Talib
source_location_hint: short sayings on forgiveness and victory

excerpt: The striving of the soul that turns toward the ḥudūd above itself is of a higher order than the striving that returns merely to one's own benefit, whether of this world or the next.
source_location_hint: on the orientations of the soul toward the higher limits

excerpt: To strive fī sabīl Allāh is the outer jihād of the self and of wealth; to strive fī Allāh is the inner recognition of the ḥudūd and the giving of their due.
source_location_hint: on the two senses of jihād

excerpt: The dog of the Companions of the Cave, stretched forth at the threshold, is the figure of the loyal dāʿī who guards the door of the Imam.
ʿAlī's lament at the grave of Fāṭima, in which he calls upon her by name and laments the impermanence of every companionship before death.
source_location_hint: on the burial of Fatima al-Zahra
